prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins on a nearly black frame, with little visible detail.

It then cuts to a modern, upscale interior with large glass panels. A woman in a swimsuit reclines on a white lounge chair, wearing large round sunglasses. She appears relaxed, lying back with her hands behind her head. A sculptural object stands to the left, and the setting has a polished, minimalist look.

A dark, close-up profile of a man follows, mostly in silhouette. The lighting obscures his facial details. The next shot shows several red Coke cans lined up in a refrigerator or bar area, with the white “Coke” lettering clearly visible. A hand reaches near the cans, and glassware is visible in front.

The scene widens again to show the same spacious room from a distance. The reclining woman is seen beyond tall window panels, with blue-toned light outside or behind the glass. A large sculptural form is silhouetted in the foreground.

A man is then shown in a medium close-up, standing indoors near flowers. He has light-colored hair and wears an open-collar shirt. He looks upward or across the room. The woman is shown in close-up: she wears reflective sunglasses, earrings, and a blue/green swimsuit or top. She touches her lips with her fingers, appearing composed and observant.

A close-up shows a hand opening a red Coke can. Condensation droplets are visible on the can surface. The woman then adjusts or lifts her sunglasses slightly, looking forward.

A hand holds a red Coke can near someone’s mouth; the person is partly obscured in shadow. The can fills much of the frame, with the “Coke” logo prominent. The man appears again in close-up, smiling slightly in low light.

The can is then shown being crushed or squeezed, with dark liquid and foam bursting or spilling out from the opened top. This is followed by a shot of the woman from behind or side-on, seated on the lounge chair with her hair backlit against the blue window light.

The next shot shows a vase of white flowers on a polished surface. Liquid splashes across or past the surface near the vase. A small dog with shaggy white and gray fur is then shown on a sofa or cushioned area, wearing a collar with a tag, looking alert.

A wide view of the room shows a grand piano at right, a stool in front of it, and a large open space with decorative objects. The lounge area and sculpture are visible in the background. A close-up of sheet music on the piano follows; a long thin object or stream crosses in front of it, but its exact source is unclear from the sampled frame.

Another close-up shows a hand holding a red Coke can on its side, with the open end visible and drops or liquid coming out. Glassware is then shown on a bar or tray: a hand places or holds a tall glass near an ice bucket or bowl of ice. A pair of sunglasses sits among the glassware. Another shot shows a long dark line or cord-like shape stretching diagonally across a pale wall or ceiling area; its function is unclear from the frames.

The woman is shown reclining again, head tilted back against the lounge chair, with blue-toned light behind her. A close-up then shows a glass being filled with dark carbonated liquid. The liquid foams heavily as it enters the glass.

The woman drinks from the foamy glass. Her face is in close-up, with the glass at her lips and foam at the top. The final frame presents a product shot against a dark background: a red Coke can stands beside a tall glass filled with dark cola and a thick pale foam head. The white “Coke” lettering on the can is clearly visible.
